text.title: ads-twitter-ads Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for ads-twitter-ads. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Use follower lookalike targeting against 3-5 specific @accounts as the primary targeting method — never rely solely on Twitter's broad interest categories for B2B or tech products.
rule.rationale: Twitter's interest taxonomy is coarser than Meta's; follower overlap against specific accounts (competitor, media, influencer) is a far more precise intent signal for niche tech and professional audiences.

rule.statement: Use keyword targeting to reach users who recently tweeted or engaged with your topic — combine with follower lookalikes to narrow to the right segment.
rule.rationale: Keyword targeting alone casts too wide a net (anyone who mentioned the word); combining with follower lookalikes ensures the keyword match occurs within an already-relevant audience.

rule.statement: Structure all Twitter/X ad copy as: Hook (stop scroll) → Value (what they get) → CTA (what to do) — never lead with brand name or product name.
rule.rationale: Twitter feed scrolls at high speed; the first line determines whether users stop. Brand-first openers are ignored; problem/benefit-first openers interrupt scrolling behavior.

rule.statement: Use conversational, text-heavy creative that looks like an organic tweet — avoid highly designed visuals with logos and taglines in the image.
rule.rationale: Twitter users have strong ad-blindness to polished promotional imagery. Native-looking ads blend into the timeline and receive 2-3x higher engagement rates than design-heavy formats on this platform.

rule.statement: Start with a minimum of $20/day; use $50+/day for meaningful testing data within 7 days.
rule.rationale: Below $20/day, impressions are too sparse to generate statistically valid A/B comparison data between ad variations in a reasonable timeframe.

rule.statement: Refresh creative every 2 weeks — Twitter audiences are more homogeneous than Meta's, causing ad fatigue faster due to high impression repetition within small niche audiences.
rule.rationale: Niche follower-lookalike audiences on Twitter can be as small as 50-200K users; at $50/day, frequency reaches 3+ within 10-14 days, after which engagement rates drop sharply.
